description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped rock with a rough, angular form and a predominantly light tan to brown coloration. The surface texture is rugged, with visible crevices, jagged edges, and some smoother, weathered planes. The rock appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or openings, and its coloration suggests sedimentary origins, possibly sandstone or limestone. Some faces are flatter and lighter, while others are darker and more textured, indicating natural weathering and exposure to the elements. The overall mass and size suggest it could serve as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban park or landscaped area. There are no visible markings, graffiti, or signs of artificial modification on its surface.
description: A large, rough, tan-brown rock with jagged edges and a rugged, weathered surface. Its solid, angular form and natural coloration make it suitable as an urban barrier or landscaping element.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest, flattest face of the rock, highlighting its light tan color and rough, uneven surface.
description_view_1: This angle reveals a more angular, pointed end with a prominent, lighter face and some darker, shadowed areas.
description_view_2: The underside and back are visible here, showing a darker, more textured surface with deeper crevices and a reddish-brown hue.
description_view_3: This view presents a tapered, elongated face with a mix of smooth and jagged surfaces, mostly in light tan with some subtle shading.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
